name: california
description: California civic and campaign finance data from official state government websites

config.description: California civic and campaign finance data from official state government websites

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract qualified ballot measures with measure ID, title, brief description, and election date
hints:
Measures are

tags containing links to ballot-measures/pdf/*.pdf

Use CSS selector 'p > a[href*="ballot-measures/pdf"]' to find measure links — the parent

is the item
The container is the main content area — no specific wrapper div exists
There are only 3 measures — do NOT match navigation links, notes, or other paragraphs
containerSelector MUST be 'body' (this page has no wrapper div); itemSelector MUST be 'p > a[href*="ballot-measures/pdf"]'

Each item is the <a> element itself. For item-scoped fieldMappings, use selector 'a' so .find()/.filter() matches the item element. Do NOT use selector 'text' or 'href' — those are interpreted as CSS tag selectors and match nothing.
Use these EXACT field names: externalId, title, detailUrl, electionDate. The consuming code ignores synonyms (id, name, link, date).

externalId (the bill code, e.g. 'ACA 13'): produce this fieldMapping verbatim — { fieldName: 'externalId', scope: 'item', selector: 'a', extractionMethod: 'regex', regexPattern: '^([A-Z]+\\s*\\d+)', regexGroup: 1, required: true }. Do NOT add a transform on this field.
title: { fieldName: 'title', scope: 'item', selector: 'a', extractionMethod: 'text', required: true }. Captures the full anchor text. Cleanup is the consumer's job; do NOT add a transform.
detailUrl: { fieldName: 'detailUrl', scope: 'item', selector: 'a', attribute: 'href', extractionMethod: 'attribute', required: true }.

electionDate: { fieldName: 'electionDate', scope: 'container', selector: 'h2', extractionMethod: 'text', transform: { type: 'date_parse', params: { format: 'MMMM D, YYYY' } } } — extracts 'November 3, 2026' from the <h2> heading above the measures.

TRANSFORMS: the only valid transform.type values are: trim, lowercase, uppercase, strip_html, url_resolve, regex_replace, name_format, date_parse. NEVER use type 'regex' — that string is not a transform. For regex-based extraction, set extractionMethod 'regex' on the field mapping itself (with regexPattern + regexGroup), as shown for externalId above.

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract scheduled Assembly committee meetings with date, time, location, and committee name
hints: Data is in structured HTML tables with embedded JSON metadata
Content is divided into committee hearings and floor calendar sections
Tables include date, time, committee name, and room location columns

Use these EXACT field names: externalId (composite e.g. 'assembly-{committee-slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}'), title (committee name + session label), body (literal 'Assembly'), scheduledAt (combined date + time), location (room), agendaUrl (if present, otherwise omit).

Prefer extractionMethod 'text' for plain values, 'attribute' for href/src, 'structured' with children for nested arrays. For substring-via-regex use extractionMethod 'regex' with regexPattern + regexGroup on the field mapping.

TRANSFORMS: the only valid transform.type values are exactly these strings: trim, lowercase, uppercase, strip_html, url_resolve, regex_replace, name_format, date_parse. NEVER invent other types (e.g., 'regex', 'extract', 'parse') — they are silently dropped at runtime.

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract Assembly members with name, district number, party affiliation, photo, and profile page link
hints: Member cards are in a grid layout, each linking to /assemblymembers/{district}
Each card has a photo, name, district, and party
Extract the member's profile page link as 'detailUrl' from the card's anchor href (e.g. /assemblymembers/30)
Use the full URL with https://www.assembly.ca.gov prefix for detailUrl if href is relative
80 Assembly members total

Use these EXACT field names: externalId, name, district (numeric string, no 'District:' prefix), party, photoUrl, detailUrl. The consuming code expects these literal names — past regenerations have stored 'District:' label text in the district field instead of the number.

externalId: produce this fieldMapping verbatim — { fieldName: 'externalId', scope: 'item', selector: 'a', attribute: 'href', extractionMethod: 'attribute', transform: { type: 'regex_replace', params: { pattern: '^.*?/assemblymembers/0?([1-9][0-9]*).*$', replacement: 'ca-assembly-$1' } }, required: true }. The pattern uses `0?([1-9][0-9]*)` — NOT `(\\d+)` — because the live site emits zero-padded hrefs for single-digit districts ('/assemblymembers/01' for district 1). `0?` consumes any optional leading zero; `[1-9][0-9]*` captures a non-zero-leading number. DO NOT 'simplify' this to `(\\d+)` — that would capture the leading zero and produce 'ca-assembly-01' instead of 'ca-assembly-1'. The captured group always contains the bare district number with NO leading zero. Examples: href '/assemblymembers/30' → externalId 'ca-assembly-30'. href '/assemblymembers/01' → externalId 'ca-assembly-1'. href '/assemblymembers/80' → externalId 'ca-assembly-80'.
Prefer extractionMethod 'text' for plain values, 'attribute' for href/src. For substring-via-regex use extractionMethod 'regex' with regexPattern + regexGroup on the field mapping.

TRANSFORMS: the only valid transform.type values are exactly these strings: trim, lowercase, uppercase, strip_html, url_resolve, regex_replace, name_format, date_parse. NEVER invent other types (e.g., 'regex', 'extract', 'parse') — they are silently dropped at runtime.

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract Senators with name, district number, party affiliation, photo, and Capitol/district office contact information
hints: BIO: do NOT extract a 'bio' field for senators. Per-senator detail sites (sr/sd{NN}.senate.ca.gov) use different Drupal themes and produce junk; the downstream BioGenerator fills bios for senators that arrive without one. The staticManifest below deliberately omits any bio mapping.

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract daily-journal PDFs from the California Assembly clerk's archive as past meeting minutes. Each session day publishes one PDF (filename pattern adj{MMDDYY}.pdf, with re-issues as adj{MMDDYY}_r{N}.pdf). The handler walks the listing descending by date and stores each PDF's text as a Minutes record; the backend's downstream linker mines the text post-sync to produce LegislativeAction rows (presence rolls, committee reports/hearings, amendments, engrossments/enrollments, resolutions) connecting representatives + propositions + committees. Lives under MEETINGS dataType — minutes are the past-record counterpart to scheduled-meeting calendar entries.
hints: Listing page is `https://clerk.assembly.ca.gov/legislative-publications/daily-journals`. PDF links live under `/sites/clerk.assembly.ca.gov/files/adj{MMDDYY}.pdf`.
The default `maxNew=10` (~2 weeks of session days) bounds cold-start ingestion. To backfill historical journals, raise `maxNew` (or wipe the watermark) for one explicit run.
Re-issues use the suffix `_r1`, `_r2`, etc.; the handler stores them as new Minutes rows with revisionSeq=N+1 and flips the original's isActive=false.

V1 stores the full PDF text on Minutes.rawText (capped at ~256kB). The backend `LegislativeActionLinkerService` mines the text after sync to produce LegislativeAction rows — no AI extraction at scrape time. AI summarization + per-passage claim attribution land in V2.

The downstream linker resolves bill citations ("Assembly Bill No. 1863" → AB 1863, normalised against `propositions.externalId`), surnames against `representatives.lastName` filtered by chamber + active status (single-match only in V1; multi-match leaves the FK null + logs), and committee names against `legislative_committees`.

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract Assembly weekly-history PDFs as bill-level activity rollups for the prior session week. Each Friday publishes one PDF covering the week's actions (committee referrals, hearings, votes, engrossments, amendments) rolled up per bill. The handler stores each PDF as a Minutes record; the downstream LegislativeActionLinkerService mines the text to produce LegislativeAction rows linked to both Bill (billId) and Proposition (propositionId, when the measure has qualified for the ballot). Companion to daily-journals — same atomic actions, different projection: journals are per-day events, histories are per-bill rollups. See opuspopuli#666.
hints: Listing page is `https://clerk.assembly.ca.gov/legislative-publications/weekly-histories`. PDF links live under `/sites/clerk.assembly.ca.gov/files/<M.D.YYYY>-weekly-history.pdf`, with re-issues suffixed `_0`, `_1`, etc.
Each `<time datetime="YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS-OFFSET">` element in the listing carries the canonical publication date; the filename's `M.D.YYYY` matches that date.
Cadence is weekly (Friday afternoon publication) — the Saturday 3 AM sync gives the upstream publisher a few hours buffer.

Histories are tabular-ish (bill ID, action, date, vote tally in columns). The same linker that handles daily journals extracts LegislativeAction rows from this PDF's text — patterns are similar enough that no parser changes are required for V1.

Cross-source dedup: the same atomic action appears in both a daily journal and that week's history. The linker dedups by (billId, date, actionType, position) tuple so the same event creates ONE LegislativeAction row, not two. See opuspopuli#666.

Bills are linked by `billNumber` match against the `Bill` table (set during the bills sync). Bills not yet ingested at history-parse time will get backfilled on the next bills sync — actions without a billId are still useful (propositionId may resolve).

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract scheduled Senate committee meetings with date, time, location, and committee name from the linked PDF
hints: This page links to a PDF — download the first .PDF link
The PDF contains committee hearing schedules organized by date
Each entry has: committee name, date, time, room/location

Use these EXACT field names: externalId ('senate-{committee-slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}', lowercase + hyphens, no spaces), title (committee name + session label), body (literal 'Senate'), scheduledAt (combined date + time), location (room).
Prefer extractionMethod 'text'. For substring-via-regex use extractionMethod 'regex' with regexPattern + regexGroup on the field mapping.

TRANSFORMS: the only valid transform.type values are exactly these strings: trim, lowercase, uppercase, strip_html, url_resolve, regex_replace, name_format, date_parse. NEVER invent other types (e.g., 'regex', 'extract', 'parse') — they are silently dropped at runtime.

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract campaign contributions from the RCPT_CD table
hints: ZIP file updated daily from CA Secretary of State, ~1GB uncompressed
RCPT_CD has 64 columns; map only the essential ones
ENTITY_CD values: IND=individual, COM=committee, OTH=other, PTY=party, SCC=small contributor
Filter to recent election cycles to manage data volume

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract campaign expenditures from the EXPN_CD table
hints: Same ZIP archive as contributions — download once, extract both tables
EXPN_CODE values: CMP=campaign paraphernalia, CNS=consulting, CTB=contribution to other committee, FIL=candidate filing fees, FND=fundraising, LIT=campaign literature, etc.

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract late independent expenditures from S496_CD
hints: S496 = Form 496 late independent expenditure reports
Filed within 24 hours of making an IE of $1,000+ in the 90 days before an election

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract ballot-measure committee declarations (Form 410) from CVR2_CAMPAIGN_DISCLOSURE_CD — links a filing (and therefore a committee) to a specific ballot measure with a support/oppose position
hints: Same dbwebexport.zip as RCPT_CD/EXPN_CD/S496_CD — only one download per sync cycle
Most CVR2 rows are entity declarations on non-ballot-measure filings; the linker filters at resolve time by ballotName/ballotNumber being non-empty and supportOrOppose being S or O
TRAN_ID is unique per record; FILING_ID + LINE_ITEM is a composite alternative if TRAN_ID ever proves unstable
SUP_OPP_CD values: S = support, O = oppose. Other values (N = neutral, blank) are skipped by the linker
Joins to existing Contribution/Expenditure rows by FILING_ID — relies on those scrapes having run first to know which committee owns each filing

dataSources.contentGoal: Discover bill status pages for the current California legislative session (both chambers). Each search result row links to a bill status page — follow those links and extract one structured Bill record per page: bill number, session year, measure type code (AB/ACA/ACR/AJR/HR/SB/SCA/SCR/SJR/SR), full title, subject, current status string, last action, last action date, fiscal impact summary, full-text URL, primary author name, co-author names, committee names, and any available roll-call vote data (member name, chamber, vote date, position, motion text).
hints: CURRENT SESSION: 2025-2026 — update the session_year URL param and this hint at the start of each new two-year legislative session
Emit sessionYear as '2025-2026' — update alongside the CURRENT SESSION hint above

leginfo bill_id format: {4-digit start year}{4-digit end year}0{MeasureType}{Number} — the '0' after the session years is always present as a revision prefix. Examples: AB 1 → 202520260AB1, SB 500 → 202520260SB500
Crawl path: search page (depth 0) → billNavClient.xhtml per bill (depth 1) → billStatusClient.xhtml + billVotesClient.xhtml (depth 2)
billStatusClient.xhtml — primary bill data: title, subject, author, co-authors, status, last action, fiscal impact, full-text link, committee referral history
billVotesClient.xhtml — roll-call votes only: member names, positions (AYE/NOE/NV/ABS/EXC), and motion text

Skip without extracting: billNavClient.xhtml (navigation hub), billHistoryClient.xhtml (history timeline), billAnalysisClient.xhtml (PDF links), billCompareClient.xhtml, billVersionsCompareClient.xhtml, billSearchClient.xhtml
Vote position symbols on billVotesClient.xhtml: AYE or Y = yes | NOE or N = no | NV = no_vote | ABS = absent | EXC = excused
Author and co-author names appear after 'Author:' and 'Coauthors:' labels on the status page — plain text, no party or district suffix
Committee referrals in history: 'Referred to Com. on JUDICIARY' → committee name is 'JUDICIARY'. Include each committee once.
Full text URL pattern: /faces/billTextClient.xhtml?bill_id={bill_id}

dataSources.contentGoal: Extract civic content from this page and any sibling pages under /resources/ — chambers and leadership, measure types and voting thresholds, ordered lifecycle stages a bill passes through, Governor action timing, glossary of legislative terms, and citizen participation guidance. Each visited page typically contributes only one slice of the CivicsBlock (e.g., the glossary page fills `glossary[]`; the legislative-process page fills `lifecycleStages[]`); the prompt-service generates plain-language rewrites alongside the verbatim source text on every field.
hints: Seed page is the canonical CA Assembly description of how a bill becomes law; sibling pages under /resources/ include the glossary and other civic-process references
Vote thresholds: 41 (Assembly majority), 21 (Senate majority), 54 (Assembly 2/3), 27 (Senate 2/3)
Governor has 12 days to sign, allow without signing, or veto; 2/3 override in each chamber
Approximately 150 glossary terms organized A-Z plus an abbreviations section — always emit BOTH `verbatim` (Assembly's exact text) AND `plainLanguage` (rewrite for a high-school-senior reading level)

Capture all measure-type abbreviations (AB, SB, ACA, SCA, ACR, SCR, AJR, SJR, HR, SR) plus high-leverage procedural terms: engrossed, enrolled, chaptered, gut and amend, urgency clause, conference committee, do pass, third reading

Status-string patterns to capture per lifecycle stage (JS regex form): 'Re-referred to Com\.', 'Set, first hearing', 'Read first time', 'Read second time', 'Read third time', 'Approved by the Governor', 'Chaptered', 'Vetoed', 'Held under submission', 'Failed passage'

Second-house stages: CalMatters API status strings 'Active Bill - In Second Committee Process' (maps to second-house-policy) and 'Active Bill - In Second House Process' (maps to second-house-fiscal). These are bill-level status strings on the CalMatters bill page, not leginfo journal entries — include them as separate statusStringPatterns on the second-house-policy and second-house-fiscal stages respectively. See OpusPopuli/opuspopuli-regions#35.

Third-reading disambiguation: the leginfo journal pattern 'Read third time' applies to FIRST-house third reading only — California's journal entries do not consistently carry a chamber prefix that distinguishes the two. If no distinct second-house third-reading pattern can be derived from the source pages, fold the third-reading-second-house stage into second-house-fiscal (i.e. omit third-reading-second-house entirely or alias its statusStringPatterns to 'Active Bill - In Second House Process'). Do NOT emit two stages with identical or near-identical patterns — the consumer uses first-match resolution and the second one is dead code.

lifecycleStageIds: every measureType's lifecycleStageIds array MUST include terminal stages — at minimum 'chaptered' and 'failed-passage' — at the end of the per-house procedural sequence. Bills that are chaptered or fail otherwise have currentStageId = null even though their status is well-defined. This is the most common gap: AB/SB measure types frequently end at 'governor-action' and omit the post-Governor terminal outcomes. Concrete shape: ['introduced', 'first-committee', ..., 'governor-action', 'chaptered', 'failed-passage']. See OpusPopuli/opuspopuli-regions#35 + opuspopuli#694.
